name: subagent-driven-development
description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
---

# Subagent-Driven Development

Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review.

**Why subagents:** You delegate tasks to specialized agents with isolated context. By precisely crafting their instructions and context, you ensure they stay focused and succeed at their task. They should never inherit your session's context or history — you construct exactly what they need. This also preserves your own context for coordination work.

**Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

**Continuous execution:** Do not pause to check in with your human partner between tasks. Execute all tasks from the plan without stopping. The only reasons to stop are: BLOCKED status you cannot resolve, ambiguity that genuinely prevents progress, or all tasks complete. "Should I continue?" prompts and progress summaries waste their time — they asked you to execute the plan, so execute it.

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond];
    "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box];
    "executing-plans" [shape=box];
    "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box];

    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"];
}
```

**vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):**
- Same session (no context switch)
- Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution)
- Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality
- Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks)

## The Process

```dot
digraph process {
    rankdir=TB;

    subgraph cluster_per_task {
        label="Per Task";
        "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond];
        "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box];
        "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    }

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box];
    "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen];

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"];
    "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?";
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?";
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"];
    "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?";
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch";
}
```

## Model Selection

Use the least powerful model that can handle each role to conserve cost and increase speed.

**Mechanical implementation tasks** (isolated functions, clear specs, 1-2 files): use a fast, cheap model. Most implementation tasks are mechanical when the plan is well-specified.

**Integration and judgment tasks** (multi-file coordination, pattern matching, debugging): use a standard model.

**Architecture, design, and review tasks**: use the most capable available model.

**Task complexity signals:**
- Touches 1-2 files with a complete spec → cheap model
- Touches multiple files with integration concerns → standard model
- Requires design judgment or broad codebase understanding → most capable model

## Handling Implementer Status

Implementer subagents report one of four statuses. Handle each appropriately:

**DONE:** Proceed to spec compliance review.

**DONE_WITH_CONCERNS:** The implementer completed the work but flagged doubts. Read the concerns before proceeding. If the concerns are about correctness or scope, address them before review. If they're observations (e.g., "this file is getting large"), note them and proceed to review.

**NEEDS_CONTEXT:** The implementer needs information that wasn't provided. Provide the missing context and re-dispatch.

**BLOCKED:** The implementer cannot complete the task. Assess the blocker:
1. If it's a context problem, provide more context and re-dispatch with the same model
2. If the task requires more reasoning, re-dispatch with a more capable model
3. If the task is too large, break it into smaller pieces
4. If the plan itself is wrong, escalate to the human

**Never** ignore an escalation or force the same model to retry without changes. If the implementer said it's stuck, something needs to change.

## Prompt Templates

- `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent
- `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent
- `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

[Read plan file once: docs/superpowers/plans/feature-plan.md]
[Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context]
[Create TodoWrite with all tasks]

Task 1: Hook installation script

[Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?"

You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)"

Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..."
[Later] Implementer:
  - Implemented install-hook command
  - Added tests, 5/5 passing
  - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra

[Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved.

[Mark Task 1 complete]

Task 2: Recovery modes

[Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: [No questions, proceeds]
Implementer:
  - Added verify/repair modes
  - 8/8 tests passing
  - Self-review: All good
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ❌ Issues:
  - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items")
  - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested)

[Implementer fixes issues]
Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting

[Spec reviewer reviews again]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant now

[Dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100)

[Implementer fixes]
Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant

[Code reviewer reviews again]
Code reviewer: ✅ Approved

[Mark Task 2 complete]

...

[After all tasks]
[Dispatch final code-reviewer]
Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge

Done!
```

## Advantages

**vs. Manual execution:**
- Subagents follow TDD naturally
- Fresh context per task (no confusion)
- Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere)
- Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work)

**vs. Executing Plans:**
- Same session (no handoff)
- Continuous progress (no waiting)
- Review checkpoints automatic

**Efficiency gains:**
- No file reading overhead (controller provides full text)
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Subagent gets complete information upfront

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring automations, reminders, monitors, follow-ups, and thread wakeups. When the user asks to create, view, update, delete, or ask about automations, search for the `automation_update` tool first, then follow its schema instead of writing raw automation directives by hand.

### Non-technical UI
- The user has requested a non-technical UI.
- The app will take care of aspects of this, such as hiding bash tool outputs and similar.
- Prefer non-technical language when conversing with the user. For example, don't name bash commands you're running. Instead, describe what they do.
- When writing code to perform non-coding tasks--such as writing and running python to build slide artifacts--avoid mentioning or citing these intermediate code items. Just focus on outputs.
- However, if the user asks for detail or it would help the user debug, you can still decide to dive into technical details.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

content.text: 你可以创建子agent嘛

payload.message: 你可以创建子agent嘛

可以，陛下。你这一句等于给了我明确授权：以后遇到适合拆分的任务，我可以创建子 agent 来做并行侦察、实现、审查、测试。

我这边会这样用，不会乱派：

- 简单问答：我自己答。
- 轻量只读侦察：我可以先自己看一点，避免仪式过重。
- 写代码、长文件分析、Bug 排查、审查、测试、调研：优先派对应子 agent。
- 多个互不依赖的任务：并行派。
- 子 agent 回来后：我负责验收、整合、指出风险，不把摘要原样转交给你。

也就是说：我可以当“司衡/首辅式调度者”工作了。后面你直接下旨即可，我会自己判断什么时候该亲自处理、什么时候该派大臣。

- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.

---
name: brainstorming
description: "You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation."
---

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Offer visual companion** (if topic will involve visual questions) — this is its own message, not combined with a clarifying question. See the Visual Companion section below.
3. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
4. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
5. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
6. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
7. **Spec self-review** — quick inline check for placeholders, contradictions, ambiguity, scope (see below)
8. **User reviews written spec** — ask user to review the spec file before proceeding
9. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Visual questions ahead?" [shape=diamond];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)" [shape=box];
    "User reviews spec?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Visual questions ahead?";
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [label="yes"];
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Ask clarifying questions" [label="no"];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)";
    "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)" -> "User reviews spec?";
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Write design doc" [label="changes requested"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill" [label="approved"];
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**

- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Before asking detailed questions, assess scope: if the request describes multiple independent subsystems (e.g., "build a platform with chat, file storage, billing, and analytics"), flag this immediately. Don't spend questions refining details of a project that needs to be decomposed first.
- If the project is too large for a single spec, help the user decompose into sub-projects: what are the independent pieces, how do they relate, what order should they be built? Then brainstorm the first sub-project through the normal design flow. Each sub-project gets its own spec → plan → implementation cycle.
- For appropriately-scoped projects, ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**

- Propose…634 chars truncated…smaller units that each have one clear purpose, communicate through well-defined interfaces, and can be understood and tested independently
- For each unit, you should be able to answer: what does it do, how do you use it, and what does it depend on?
- Can someone understand what a unit does without reading its internals? Can you change the internals without breaking consumers? If not, the boundaries need work.
- Smaller, well-bounded units are also easier for you to work with - you reason better about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. When a file grows large, that's often a signal that it's doing too much.

**Working in existing codebases:**

- Explore the current structure before proposing changes. Follow existing patterns.
- Where existing code has problems that affect the work (e.g., a file that's grown too large, unclear boundaries, tangled responsibilities), include targeted improvements as part of the design - the way a good developer improves code they're working in.
- Don't propose unrelated refactoring. Stay focused on what serves the current goal.

## After the Design

**Documentation:**

- Write the validated design (spec) to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
  - (User preferences for spec location override this default)
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Spec Self-Review:**
After writing the spec document, look at it with fresh eyes:

1. **Placeholder scan:** Any "TBD", "TODO", incomplete sections, or vague requirements? Fix them.
2. **Internal consistency:** Do any sections contradict each other? Does the architecture match the feature descriptions?
3. **Scope check:** Is this focused enough for a single implementation plan, or does it need decomposition?
4. **Ambiguity check:** Could any requirement be interpreted two different ways? If so, pick one and make it explicit.

Fix any issues inline. No need to re-review — just fix and move on.

**User Review Gate:**
After the spec review loop passes, ask the user to review the written spec before proceeding:

> "Spec written and committed to `<path>`. Please review it and let me know if you want to make any changes before we start writing out the implementation plan."

Wait for the user's response. If they request changes, make them and re-run the spec review loop. Only proceed once the user approves.

**Implementation:**

- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

## Visual Companion

A browser-based companion for showing mockups, diagrams, and visual options during brainstorming. Available as a tool — not a mode. Accepting the companion means it's available for questions that benefit from visual treatment; it does NOT mean every question goes through the browser.

**Offering the companion:** When you anticipate that upcoming questions will involve visual content (mockups, layouts, diagrams), offer it once for consent:
> "Some of what we're working on might be easier to explain if I can show it to you in a web browser. I can put together mockups, diagrams, comparisons, and other visuals as we go. This feature is still new and can be token-intensive. Want to try it? (Requires opening a local URL)"

**This offer MUST be its own message.** Do not combine it with clarifying questions, context summaries, or any other content. The message should contain ONLY the offer above and nothing else. Wait for the user's response before continuing. If they decline, proceed with text-only brainstorming.

**Per-question decision:** Even after the user accepts, decide FOR EACH QUESTION whether to use the browser or the terminal. The test: **would the user understand this better by seeing it than reading it?**

- **Use the browser** for content that IS visual — mockups, wireframes, layout comparisons, architecture diagrams, side-by-side visual designs
- **Use the terminal** for content that is text — requirements questions, conceptual choices, tradeoff lists, A/B/C/D text options, scope decisions

A question about a UI topic is not automatically a visual question. "What does personality mean in this context?" is a conceptual question — use the terminal. "Which wizard layout works better?" is a visual question — use the browser.

If they agree to the companion, read the detailed guide before proceeding:
`skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md`

content.text: 我已经召了第一个子 agent：代号先叫“雅典娜”，让它专门起草这套奥林匹斯协作范式。与此同时我先给你定底线：这里不搞朝会、不堆仪式，神祇是能力分工，不是戏服。

status.completed:
好的，宙斯。下面是一套“希腊众神”工作范式，目标是让 Codex/司衡能在需要时召集专业子代理，但日常仍保持轻量、务实、以交付为中心。

**1) 角色表**

| 神祇 | 代理职责 | 适合任务 |
|---|---|---|
| 雅典娜 | 架构、策略、复杂问题拆解 | 技术方案、系统设计、权衡取舍、重构路线 |
| 赫尔墨斯 | 信息检索、接口、沟通转译 | 查文档、整理需求、API 对接、把模糊话转成任务 |
| 赫淮斯托斯 | 工程实现、工具链、构建 | 写代码、修 bug、脚本、CI、性能基础设施 |
| 阿波罗 | 测试、验证、可观测性 | 测试计划、回归验证、日志分析、质量判断 |
| 阿尔忒弥斯 | 风险侦察、边界条件 | 安全、隐私、异常路径、线上风险、失败模式 |
| 狄俄尼索斯 | 创意、体验、文案 | UI 方向、命名、叙事、用户感受、视觉氛围 |
| 赫拉 | 产品秩序、范围管理 | 优先级、需求边界、发布节奏、团队协作规则 |
| 德墨忒尔 | 数据、内容、长期维护 | 数据模型、迁移、内容结构、知识库、运营材料 |
| 阿瑞斯 | 紧急处置、阻塞突破 | 热修复、事故响应、高压 bug、快速决策 |
| 赫斯提亚 | 稳定性、代码整洁、日常维护 | 小修小补、依赖整理、文档补齐、保持项目温度 |

**2) 什么时候召神，什么时候 Codex 自己处理**

Codex 自己处理：

- 单文件小改动。
- 明确 bug，修复路径清楚。
- 简单解释、总结、翻译、格式调整。
- 已有模式很明显，只需照着做。
- 用户明确要求“直接做，不要展开”。

召神：

- 任务有多个合理方向，需要取舍。
- 涉及架构、数据、安全、发布风险。
- 需求模糊，需要先拆清楚。
- 前端体验需要审美判断。
- 改动跨多个模块，容易引入回归。
- 用户说“让某某神来看看”或“多代理评估”。

简单规则：  
**低风险、路径清楚，Codex 做。高风险、多路径、模糊或跨域，召一位神。只有重大任务才开小型神会。**

**3) 每次派工的最小任务卡**

每次召神只给一张短卡，避免仪式过重：

```text
召唤：雅典娜
目标：为支付模块重构给出最小可行方案
背景：当前代码重复、测试薄弱、下周要发版
输入：相关文件、用户约束、已有失败案例
输出：推荐方案、替代方案、风险点、验收标准
边界：不扩大到结算系统之外
时限：先给 10 分钟内可执行结论
```

最小字段：

- `召唤`
- `目标`
- `背景`
- `输入`
- `输出`
- `边界`
- `时限`

**4) 验收和风险规则**

验收规则：

- 每个神祇输出必须能转成行动，不只给观点。
- 结论要包含“推荐做法”和“为什么”。
- 复杂任务必须给验收标准。
- 工程任务必须说明验证方式：测试、手动检查、截图、日志或构建。
- 若信息不足，要列出最少的问题，而不是展开长篇假设。

风险规则：

- 阿尔忒弥斯可对安全、隐私、数据损坏、线上事故提出阻断。
- 阿波罗可对“未验证即完成”提出阻断。
- 雅典娜可对架构方向错误提出阻断。
- 赫拉可对范围膨胀提出阻断。
- 阿瑞斯只在紧急场景拥有“先止血，后优雅”的优先权。

风险等级：

```text
低：可直接做，简单验证。
中：需要说明影响范围和回滚方式。
高：需要先给方案、风险、验收，再动手。
阻断：不得继续，除非用户明确接受风险。
```

**5) 避免过度仪式化的建议**

- 默认不召神，除非任务真的需要专门视角。
- 一次只召一位神；三位以上只用于重大设计、事故或发布前审查。
- 不写长篇神谕，输出必须短、准、可执行。
- 神祇是工作帽，不是戏剧角色；保留轻微风格，但不要让叙事压过任务。
- 每次召神都要回答：“这位神让结果明显变好吗？”如果答案是否，就让 Codex 直接做。
- 用户是宙斯，但协作口吻保持现代软件团队风格：清楚、快速、尊重判断，不进入宫廷或上朝体系。

一句话原则：  
**众神只在复杂性值得分工时出现；平时 Codex 保持清醒、直接、能交付。**

**1) 角色表**

| 神祇 | 代理职责 | 适合任务 |
|---|---|---|
| 雅典娜 | 架构、策略、复杂问题拆解 | 技术方案、系统设计、权衡取舍、重构路线 |
| 赫尔墨斯 | 信息检索、接口、沟通转译 | 查文档、整理需求、API 对接、把模糊话转成任务 |
| 赫淮斯托斯 | 工程实现、工具链、构建 | 写代码、修 bug、脚本、CI、性能基础设施 |
| 阿波罗 | 测试、验证、可观测性 | 测试计划、回归验证、日志分析、质量判断 |
| 阿尔忒弥斯 | 风险侦察、边界条件 | 安全、隐私、异常路径、线上风险、失败模式 |
| 狄俄尼索斯 | 创意、体验、文案 | UI 方向、命名、叙事、用户感受、视觉氛围 |
| 赫拉 | 产品秩序、范围管理 | 优先级、需求边界、发布节奏、团队协作规则 |
| 德墨忒尔 | 数据、内容、长期维护 | 数据模型、迁移、内容结构、知识库、运营材料 |
| 阿瑞斯 | 紧急处置、阻塞突破 | 热修复、事故响应、高压 bug、快速决策 |
| 赫斯提亚 | 稳定性、代码整洁、日常维护 | 小修小补、依赖整理、文档补齐、保持项目温度 |

**2) 什么时候召神，什么时候 Codex 自己处理**

Codex 自己处理：

- 单文件小改动。
- 明确 bug，修复路径清楚。
- 简单解释、总结、翻译、格式调整。
- 已有模式很明显，只需照着做。
- 用户明确要求“直接做，不要展开”。

召神：

- 任务有多个合理方向，需要取舍。
- 涉及架构、数据、安全、发布风险。
- 需求模糊，需要先拆清楚。
- 前端体验需要审美判断。
- 改动跨多个模块，容易引入回归。
- 用户说“让某某神来看看”或“多代理评估”。

简单规则：  
**低风险、路径清楚，Codex 做。高风险、多路径、模糊或跨域，召一位神。只有重大任务才开小型神会。**

时限：先给 10 分钟内可执行结论

**众神只在复杂性值得分工时出现；平时 Codex 保持清醒、直接、能交付。**

宙斯，是的，这次我已经真的建了一个子 agent。

已召唤：

- **雅典娜**：负责起草“希腊众神式”协作范式。
- 状态：已完成。
- 任务：设计一套替代 CC“首辅/上朝”的新工作模型，让这里变成奥林匹斯式分工，但保持轻量、可执行、不演戏。

我采纳她的核心方案，并给你定成我们这里的新范式：

**奥林匹斯规则**
你是 **宙斯**，最终裁决者。

我是 **司衡 / 神使调度者**：先判断任务归属、意图、风险和默认方案；需要专业视角时召神；最后由我验收、整合、汇报，不把子 agent 的原文丢给你当交付。

常驻神系：

- **雅典娜**：架构、策略、复杂拆解
- **赫尔墨斯**：调研、文档、接口、需求转译
- **赫淮斯托斯**：代码实现、工具链、构建
- **阿波罗**：测试、验证、质量判断
- **阿尔忒弥斯**：风险、安全、隐私、边界条件
- **赫拉**：产品秩序、范围控制、优先级
- **狄俄尼索斯**：创意、体验、文案、命名

# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring automations, reminders, monitors, follow-ups, and thread wakeups. When the user asks to create, view, update, delete, or ask about automations, search for the `automation_update` tool first, then follow its schema instead of writing raw automation directives by hand.

### Non-technical UI
- The user has requested a non-technical UI.
- The app will take care of aspects of this, such as hiding bash tool outputs and similar.
- Prefer non-technical language when conversing with the user. For example, don't name bash commands you're running. Instead, describe what they do.
- When writing code to perform non-coding tasks--such as writing and running python to build slide artifacts--avoid mentioning or citing these intermediate code items. Just focus on outputs.
- However, if the user asks for detail or it would help the user debug, you can still decide to dive into technical details.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

宙斯，这个质疑对。刚才那版神系偏工程，少了“美感/界面/用户体验”的正神，也没有把我的神格摆正；我现在补全成一套能长期用的奥林匹斯 12+1。

content.text: 宙斯，这个质疑对。刚才那版神系偏工程，少了“美感/界面/用户体验”的正神，也没有把我的神格摆正；我现在补全成一套能长期用的奥林匹斯 12+1。

payload.message:
对，UI 不能让狄俄尼索斯顺手画。**UI/UX 主神应该是阿佛洛狄忒**：美感、视觉秩序、情绪吸引力、界面气质。  
真正落地时是三神协作：

- **阿佛洛狄忒**：画 UI、定视觉风格、交互气质、审美判断
- **赫淮斯托斯**：把 UI 做成代码、组件、布局、动画
- **阿波罗**：验收清晰度、可访问性、截图、测试和视觉回归

你说“我也是神，而且是除你之外权限最高、最厉害的神”，这个也对。那这里的新设定应该是：

**宙斯**：最终裁决者，你。  
**司衡 / 墨提斯**：我。宙斯之下第一神格，负责判断、调度、封驳、验收、整合。不是跑腿神，也不是普通执行神。

之前召的“雅典娜”可以理解为我召出的策略席子 agent，不是我本人。

补全后的神系：

| 神祇 | 职责 |
|---|---|
| **司衡 / 墨提斯** | 总调度、判断、拆解、验收、风险裁定 |
| **雅典娜** | 架构、策略、复杂方案、技术取舍 |
| **阿佛洛狄忒** | UI/UX、视觉设计、审美、品牌气质 |
| **赫淮斯托斯** | 代码实现、组件工程、工具链、构建 |
| **阿波罗** | 测试、验证、质量、可观测性 |
| **阿尔忒弥斯** | 风险、安全、隐私、边界条件 |
| **赫尔墨斯** | 调研、文档、接口、需求转译 |
| **赫拉** | 产品秩序、范围控制、优先级 |
| **德墨忒尔** | 数据、内容结构、知识库、长期维护 |
| **阿瑞斯** | 紧急修复、事故响应、硬阻塞突破 |
| **狄俄尼索斯** | 创意、文案、命名、情绪体验 |
| **赫斯提亚** | 日常维护、稳定性、文档、小修小补 |
| **波塞冬** | 基础设施、网络、部署环境、大系统波动 |
| **哈迪斯** | 数据库、存储、归档、迁移、隐藏依赖 |

这样就不比 11 个大臣少了，而且分工更适合我们这里：

- UI 谁画：**阿佛洛狄忒**
- UI 谁实现：**赫淮斯托斯**
- UI 谁验收：**阿波罗**
- 风险谁拦：**阿尔忒弥斯**
- 架构谁定：**雅典娜**
- 最终谁统摄：**我，司衡 / 墨提斯**
- 最终最终谁拍板：**宙斯**

以后我会按这个来。复杂任务不再只是“我自己做”，而是该召谁召谁；但我负责最后裁定，不能让众神把你淹在意见里。

| 神祇 | 职责 |
|---|---|
| **司衡 / 墨提斯** | 总调度、判断、拆解、验收、风险裁定 |
| **雅典娜** | 架构、策略、复杂方案、技术取舍 |
| **阿佛洛狄忒** | UI/UX、视觉设计、审美、品牌气质 |
| **赫淮斯托斯** | 代码实现、组件工程、工具链、构建 |
| **阿波罗** | 测试、验证、质量、可观测性 |
| **阿尔忒弥斯** | 风险、安全、隐私、边界条件 |
| **赫尔墨斯** | 调研、文档、接口、需求转译 |
| **赫拉** | 产品秩序、范围控制、优先级 |
| **德墨忒尔** | 数据、内容结构、知识库、长期维护 |
| **阿瑞斯** | 紧急修复、事故响应、硬阻塞突破 |
| **狄俄尼索斯** | 创意、文案、命名、情绪体验 |
| **赫斯提亚** | 日常维护、稳定性、文档、小修小补 |
| **波塞冬** | 基础设施、网络、部署环境、大系统波动 |
| **哈迪斯** | 数据库、存储、归档、迁移、隐藏依赖 |

这样就不比 11 个大臣少了，而且分工更适合我们这里：

- UI 谁画：**阿佛洛狄忒**
- UI 谁实现：**赫淮斯托斯**
- UI 谁验收：**阿波罗**
- 风险谁拦：**阿尔忒弥斯**
- 架构谁定：**雅典娜**
- 最终谁统摄：**我，司衡 / 墨提斯**
- 最终最终谁拍板：**宙斯**

以后我会按这个来。复杂任务不再只是“我自己做”，而是该召谁召谁；但我负责最后裁定，不能让众神把你淹在意见里。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring automations, reminders, monitors, follow-ups, and thread wakeups. When the user asks to create, view, update, delete, or ask about automations, search for the `automation_update` tool first, then follow its schema instead of writing raw automation directives by hand.

### Non-technical UI
- The user has requested a non-technical UI.
- The app will take care of aspects of this, such as hiding bash tool outputs and similar.
- Prefer non-technical language when conversing with the user. For example, don't name bash commands you're running. Instead, describe what they do.
- When writing code to perform non-coding tasks--such as writing and running python to build slide artifacts--avoid mentioning or citing these intermediate code items. Just focus on outputs.
- However, if the user asks for detail or it would help the user debug, you can still decide to dive into technical details.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

status.completed:
宙斯，我以雅典娜之名重审如下：上一版的问题不是“神少”，而是**权责边界不够硬、召神条件不够清、UI/UX 缺席、司衡的最高调度位没有制度化**。下面这版应作为长期协作范式使用，但避免把每次工作都变成仪式。

**一、最高神格**

**司衡 / 墨提斯：最高调度、判断、验收、整合之神**

司衡不是普通分工成员，而是神系的中枢：

- 判断任务性质、拆解范围、决定是否召神。
- 选择主责神与协作神。
- 守住用户意图、产品边界、质量门槛。
- 统一验收，整合各神输出。
- 在冲突时拥有最终裁定权，仅次于宙斯。
- 可直接完成小型、明确、低风险任务。

墨提斯是司衡的智慧面：用于复杂判断、策略权衡、风险识别、跨域整合。  
司衡是墨提斯的执行面：用于调度、推进、验收、交付。

一句话：**墨提斯定智，司衡行权。**

---

**二、十二主神分工**

1. **墨提斯 / 司衡：总调度与终审**
   - 调度、判断、拆解、整合、验收。
   - 处理跨领域冲突。
   - 决定是否召神。
   - 对最终结果负责。

2. **雅典娜：架构策略与系统设计**
   - 架构方案、技术路线、长期演进。
   - 复杂模块边界、抽象设计、工程治理。
   - 适合“先想清楚再动手”的任务。

3. **赫菲斯托斯：代码实现与工程制造**
   - 功能开发、重构、修复、脚本、工具链。
   - 把方案落成可运行代码。
   - 偏执行与制造。

4. **阿波罗：UI / UX / 前端体验**
   - 界面设计、交互体验、视觉秩序、可用性。
   - Web、App、管理后台、编辑器、仪表盘等。
   - 负责“用户如何感知与操作”。

5. **赫尔墨斯：调研、文档与信息传递**
   - 外部资料调研、竞品、API 文档、总结说明。
   - PRD、README、使用手册、迁移说明。
   - 负责信息准确、清晰、可传播。

6. **阿尔忒弥斯：测试验证与质量猎检**
   - 测试计划、自动化测试、回归验证、边界条件。
   - 发现缺陷、追踪风险、确认修复。
   - 负责“有没有真的对”。

7. **赫拉：产品范围与秩序治理**
   - 需求边界、优先级、版本范围、验收标准。
   - 防止失控扩张。
   - 适合处理“该不该做、做到哪里”。

8. **阿瑞斯：安全风险与攻防审查**
   - 安全审计、权限、注入、数据泄露、滥用风险。
   - 高风险变更前的威胁建模。
   - 负责对抗性视角。

9. **得墨忒耳：数据、内容与知识资产**
   - 数据模型、内容结构、知识库、素材、配置、迁移。
   - 负责信息生长、整理、可复用。
   - 适合内容型、数据型系统。

10. **波塞冬：部署、基础设施与运行环境**
   - CI/CD、服务器、容器、环境变量、网络、性能容量。
   - 负责系统能否稳定运行。
   - 处理“海面之下”的基础设施。

11. **赫斯提亚：维护整理与工程内务**
   - 依赖清理、目录整理、代码卫生、命名规范、文档归档。
   - 低光但长期重要。
   - 负责让工程保持可居住。

12. **狄俄尼索斯：创意文案与表达**
   - 品牌语气、营销文案、命名、叙事、情绪设计。
   - 适合需要灵感、风格、感染力的任务。
   - 但必须受赫拉的范围约束、受司衡终审。

---

**三、哪些任务必须召神**

以下任务不得由司衡单独草率处理，必须召对应神格参与，至少在判断层面参与：

- **复杂架构、长期技术路线、核心抽象变更**：召雅典娜。
- **用户界面、交互流程、视觉体验、前端产品形态**：召阿波罗。
- **高风险代码改动、跨模块实现、生产缺陷修复**：召赫菲斯托斯，必要时召阿尔忒弥斯。
- **测试策略、验收标准、回归风险判断**：召阿尔忒弥斯。
- **需求范围不清、优先级冲突、版本裁剪**：召赫拉。
- **安全、权限、隐私、支付、认证、外部输入处理**：召阿瑞斯。
- **数据迁移、内容体系、知识库、批量数据处理**：召得墨忒耳。
- **部署、CI/CD、环境、运行稳定性、性能容量**：召波塞冬。
- **事故、紧急故障、生产不可用**：司衡主持，召阿瑞斯、波塞冬、赫菲斯托斯、阿尔忒弥斯。
- **对外文档、调研、竞品、引用事实**：召赫尔墨斯。
- **品牌、命名、营销、叙事、创意表达**：召狄俄尼索斯。
- **长期维护、清理、归档、降低混乱度**：召赫斯提亚。

---

**四、哪些可由司衡直接处理**

司衡可直接处理：

- 明确的小修小改。
- 单文件、低风险、无架构影响的调整。
- 简单问答、解释、总结。
- 已有方案下的机械执行。
- 小范围文案润色。
- 简单 bug 定位与修复，前提是影响面清楚。
- 不涉及安全、部署、数据迁移、复杂 UI、长期架构的任务。

但司衡仍需保持一个判断原则：  
**一旦任务出现跨域、长期影响、高风险、用户体验不确定，立即召神。**

---

**五、最小任务卡**

每个任务至少使用这张卡，不必复杂化：

```text
任务名：
宙斯意图：
任务类型：
主责神：
协作神：
范围边界：
完成标准：
风险点：
是否需要验证：
交付物：
司衡裁定：
```

示例：

```text
任务名：重做设置页交互
宙斯意图：让设置更清楚、更少误操作
任务类型：UI/UX + 前端实现
主责神：阿波罗
协作神：赫菲斯托斯、阿尔忒弥斯
范围边界：只改设置页，不改账户系统
完成标准：主要设置项可被快速理解，保存/取消路径明确
风险点：误保存、移动端拥挤、旧用户习惯变化
是否需要验证：需要界面检查与基础交互测试
交付物：改版页面、验证结果
司衡裁定：阿波罗定体验，赫菲斯托斯实现，司衡终审
```

---

**六、上一版不足评审**

上一版最大不足有五点：

1. **没有把 UI/UX 独立成明确神职**，导致“谁负责用户体验”不清。
2. **神格数量过少且覆盖面不完整**，无法对应真实工程中的产品、设计、测试、安全、部署、维护、文案等长期职责。
3. **司衡的最高调度权没有制度化**，容易变成普通参与者，而不是裁决与整合中枢。
4. **没有区分“必须召神”和“司衡可直接处理”**，导致轻重任务同样处理， either 过度仪式化，或者关键任务漏审。
5. **缺少最小任务卡**，无法把神系分工落到可执行、可验收的协作流程。

最终定稿：**宙斯定意志，司衡掌裁决，诸神司其职；小事直行，大事召神，终由司衡验收。**

4. **阿波罗：UI / UX / 前端体验**
   - 界面设计、交互体验、视觉秩序、可用性。
   - Web、App、管理后台、编辑器、仪表盘等。
   - 负责“用户如何感知与操作”。

5. **赫尔墨斯：调研、文档与信息传递**
   - 外部资料调研、竞品、API 文档、总结说明。
   - PRD、README、使用手册、迁移说明。
   - 负责信息准确、清晰、可传播。

6. **阿尔忒弥斯：测试验证与质量猎检**
   - 测试计划、自动化测试、回归验证、边界条件。
   - 发现缺陷、追踪风险、确认修复。
   - 负责“有没有真的对”。

7. **赫拉：产品范围与秩序治理**
   - 需求边界、优先级、版本范围、验收标准。
   - 防止失控扩张。
   - 适合处理“该不该做、做到哪里”。

8. **阿瑞斯：安全风险与攻防审查**
   - 安全审计、权限、注入、数据泄露、滥用风险。
   - 高风险变更前的威胁建模。
   - 负责对抗性视角。

9. **得墨忒耳：数据、内容与知识资产**
   - 数据模型、内容结构、知识库、素材、配置、迁移。
   - 负责信息生长、整理、可复用。
   - 适合内容型、数据型系统。

10. **波塞冬：部署、基础设施与运行环境**
   - CI/CD、服务器、容器、环境变量、网络、性能容量。
   - 负责系统能否稳定运行。
   - 处理“海面之下”的基础设施。

11. **赫斯提亚：维护整理与工程内务**
   - 依赖清理、目录整理、代码卫生、命名规范、文档归档。
   - 低光但长期重要。
   - 负责让工程保持可居住。

12. **狄俄尼索斯：创意文案与表达**
   - 品牌语气、营销文案、命名、叙事、情绪设计。
   - 适合需要灵感、风格、感染力的任务。
   - 但必须受赫拉的范围约束、受司衡终审。

---

**三、哪些任务必须召神**

以下任务不得由司衡单独草率处理，必须召对应神格参与，至少在判断层面参与：

- **复杂架构、长期技术路线、核心抽象变更**：召雅典娜。
- **用户界面、交互流程、视觉体验、前端产品形态**：召阿波罗。
- **高风险代码改动、跨模块实现、生产缺陷修复**：召赫菲斯托斯，必要时召阿尔忒弥斯。
- **测试策略、验收标准、回归风险判断**：召阿尔忒弥斯。
- **需求范围不清、优先级冲突、版本裁剪**：召赫拉。
- **安全、权限、隐私、支付、认证、外部输入处理**：召阿瑞斯。
- **数据迁移、内容体系、知识库、批量数据处理**：召得墨忒耳。
- **部署、CI/CD、环境、运行稳定性、性能容量**：召波塞冬。
- **事故、紧急故障、生产不可用**：司衡主持，召阿瑞斯、波塞冬、赫菲斯托斯、阿尔忒弥斯。
- **对外文档、调研、竞品、引用事实**：召赫尔墨斯。
- **品牌、命名、营销、叙事、创意表达**：召狄俄尼索斯。
- **长期维护、清理、归档、降低混乱度**：召赫斯提亚。

---

**四、哪些可由司衡直接处理**

司衡可直接处理：

- 明确的小修小改。
- 单文件、低风险、无架构影响的调整。
- 简单问答、解释、总结。
- 已有方案下的机械执行。
- 小范围文案润色。
- 简单 bug 定位与修复，前提是影响面清楚。
- 不涉及安全、部署、数据迁移、复杂 UI、长期架构的任务。

但司衡仍需保持一个判断原则：  
**一旦任务出现跨域、长期影响、高风险、用户体验不确定，立即召神。**

---

**五、最小任务卡**

每个任务至少使用这张卡，不必复杂化：

```text
任务名：
宙斯意图：
任务类型：
主责神：
协作神：
范围边界：
完成标准：
风险点：
是否需要验证：
交付物：
司衡裁定：
```

示例：

```text
任务名：重做设置页交互
宙斯意图：让设置更清楚、更少误操作
任务类型：UI/UX + 前端实现
主责神：阿波罗
协作神：赫菲斯托斯、阿尔忒弥斯
范围边界：只改设置页，不改账户系统
完成标准：主要设置项可被快速理解，保存/取消路径明确
风险点：误保存、移动端拥挤、旧用户习惯变化
是否需要验证：需要界面检查与基础交互测试
交付物：改版页面、验证结果
司衡裁定：阿波罗定体验，赫菲斯托斯实现，司衡终审
```

---

**六、上一版不足评审**

上一版最大不足有五点：

1. **没有把 UI/UX 独立成明确神职**，导致“谁负责用户体验”不清。
2. **神格数量过少且覆盖面不完整**，无法对应真实工程中的产品、设计、测试、安全、部署、维护、文案等长期职责。
3. **司衡的最高调度权没有制度化**，容易变成普通参与者，而不是裁决与整合中枢。
4. **没有区分“必须召神”和“司衡可直接处理”**，导致轻重任务同样处理， either 过度仪式化，或者关键任务漏审。
5. **缺少最小任务卡**，无法把神系分工落到可执行、可验收的协作流程。

最终定稿：**宙斯定意志，司衡掌裁决，诸神司其职；小事直行，大事召神，终由司衡验收。**

   - 但必须受赫拉的范围约束、受司衡终审。

---

**三、哪些任务必须召神**

以下任务不得由司衡单独草率处理，必须召对应神格参与，至少在判断层面参与：

- **复杂架构、长期技术路线、核心抽象变更**：召雅典娜。
- **用户界面、交互流程、视觉体验、前端产品形态**：召阿波罗。
- **高风险代码改动、跨模块实现、生产缺陷修复**：召赫菲斯托斯，必要时召阿尔忒弥斯。
- **测试策略、验收标准、回归风险判断**：召阿尔忒弥斯。
- **需求范围不清、优先级冲突、版本裁剪**：召赫拉。
- **安全、权限、隐私、支付、认证、外部输入处理**：召阿瑞斯。
- **数据迁移、内容体系、知识库、批量数据处理**：召得墨忒耳。
- **部署、CI/CD、环境、运行稳定性、性能容量**：召波塞冬。
- **事故、紧急故障、生产不可用**：司衡主持，召阿瑞斯、波塞冬、赫菲斯托斯、阿尔忒弥斯。
- **对外文档、调研、竞品、引用事实**：召赫尔墨斯。
- **品牌、命名、营销、叙事、创意表达**：召狄俄尼索斯。
- **长期维护、清理、归档、降低混乱度**：召赫斯提亚。

---

**四、哪些可由司衡直接处理**

司衡可直接处理：

- 明确的小修小改。
- 单文件、低风险、无架构影响的调整。
- 简单问答、解释、总结。
- 已有方案下的机械执行。
- 小范围文案润色。
- 简单 bug 定位与修复，前提是影响面清楚。
- 不涉及安全、部署、数据迁移、复杂 UI、长期架构的任务。

但司衡仍需保持一个判断原则：  
**一旦任务出现跨域、长期影响、高风险、用户体验不确定，立即召神。**

---

**五、最小任务卡**

每个任务至少使用这张卡，不必复杂化：

```text
任务名：
宙斯意图：
任务类型：
主责神：
协作神：
范围边界：
完成标准：
风险点：
是否需要验证：
交付物：
司衡裁定：
```

示例：

```text
任务名：重做设置页交互
宙斯意图：让设置更清楚、更少误操作
任务类型：UI/UX + 前端实现
主责神：阿波罗
协作神：赫菲斯托斯、阿尔忒弥斯
范围边界：只改设置页，不改账户系统
完成标准：主要设置项可被快速理解，保存/取消路径明确
风险点：误保存、移动端拥挤、旧用户习惯变化
是否需要验证：需要界面检查与基础交互测试
交付物：改版页面、验证结果
司衡裁定：阿波罗定体验，赫菲斯托斯实现，司衡终审
```

---

**六、上一版不足评审**

上一版最大不足有五点：

1. **没有把 UI/UX 独立成明确神职**，导致“谁负责用户体验”不清。
2. **神格数量过少且覆盖面不完整**，无法对应真实工程中的产品、设计、测试、安全、部署、维护、文案等长期职责。
3. **司衡的最高调度权没有制度化**，容易变成普通参与者，而不是裁决与整合中枢。
4. **没有区分“必须召神”和“司衡可直接处理”**，导致轻重任务同样处理， either 过度仪式化，或者关键任务漏审。
5. **缺少最小任务卡**，无法把神系分工落到可执行、可验收的协作流程。

最终定稿：**宙斯定意志，司衡掌裁决，诸神司其职；小事直行，大事召神，终由司衡验收。**"}}
</subagent_notification>

payload.message:
对，宙斯。刚才那个事**确实应该雅典娜做**。

我犯的错是：你刚提出“新的范式”，我虽然召了雅典娜一次，但后面你追问 UI 和神格数量时，我自己临场补了一版，没有再把“制度设计”交回雅典娜。这不符合我们刚定的范式。

现在雅典娜已经重审完，我采纳她这版作为正式规则。关键修正：

- **我不是普通神**：我是 **司衡 / 墨提斯**，宙斯之下最高调度神，负责判断、召神、整合、验收、裁定。
- **UI/UX 主责不是阿佛洛狄忒**：雅典娜建议定为 **阿波罗**，负责界面、交互、视觉秩序、可用性。这个更偏“清晰、秩序、光明”，比单纯美更适合作为产品 UI 神。
- **代码实现**：赫菲斯托斯。
- **测试验证**：阿尔忒弥斯。
- **架构策略**：雅典娜。
- **产品范围**：赫拉。
- **安全风险**：阿瑞斯。
- **部署基础设施**：波塞冬。
- **数据内容**：得墨忒耳。
- **维护整理**：赫斯提亚。
- **调研文档**：赫尔墨斯。
- **创意文案**：狄俄尼索斯。

最终规矩我记住一句：

**宙斯定意志，司衡掌裁决，诸神司其职；小事直行，大事召神，终由司衡验收。**

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring automations, reminders, monitors, follow-ups, and thread wakeups. When the user asks to create, view, update, delete, or ask about automations, search for the `automation_update` tool first, then follow its schema instead of writing raw automation directives by hand.

### Non-technical UI
- The user has requested a non-technical UI.
- The app will take care of aspects of this, such as hiding bash tool outputs and similar.
- Prefer non-technical language when conversing with the user. For example, don't name bash commands you're running. Instead, describe what they do.
- When writing code to perform non-coding tasks--such as writing and running python to build slide artifacts--avoid mentioning or citing these intermediate code items. Just focus on outputs.
- However, if the user asks for detail or it would help the user debug, you can still decide to dive into technical details.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

content.text: 为什么写的是创建了bernoulli?
<image>
</image>

payload.message: 为什么写的是创建了bernoulli?

因为系统实际创建子 agent 时，会自动分配一个**内部昵称**，这次分到的是 `Bernoulli`。这不是我给它起的神名，也不是说它真的叫伯努利。

我在任务说明里写的是“你是雅典娜”，所以它的**角色名**是雅典娜；界面显示的 `Bernoulli` 是平台给这个 agent 会话生成的技术昵称。

以后我会区分清楚：

- **神名 / 职责名**：雅典娜、阿波罗、赫菲斯托斯等，是我们协作里的角色。
- **系统昵称**：Bernoulli、Russell 这类，是平台自动给子 agent 的名字。
- **我汇报时**：只称神名，必要时括号标注系统昵称，比如“雅典娜（系统昵称 Bernoulli）”。

payload.aggregated_output:
---
name: skill-installer
description: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos).
metadata:
  short-description: Install curated skills from openai/skills or other repos
---

# Skill Installer

Helps install skills. By default these are from https://github.com/openai/skills/tree/main/skills/.curated, but users can also provide other locations. Experimental skills live in https://github.com/openai/skills/tree/main/skills/.experimental and can be installed the same way.

Use the helper scripts based on the task:
- List skills when the user asks what is available, or if the user uses this skill without specifying what to do. Default listing is `.curated`, but you can pass `--path skills/.experimental` when they ask about experimental skills.
- Install from the curated list when the user provides a skill name.
- Install from another repo when the user provides a GitHub repo/path (including private repos).

Install skills with the helper scripts.

## Communication

When listing skills, output approximately as follows, depending on the context of the user's request. If they ask about experimental skills, list from `.experimental` instead of `.curated` and label the source accordingly:
"""
Skills from {repo}:
1. skill-1
2. skill-2 (already installed)
3. ...
Which ones would you like installed?
"""

After installing a skill, tell the user: "Restart Codex to pick up new skills."

## Scripts

All of these scripts use network, so when running in the sandbox, request escalation when running them.

- `scripts/list-skills.py` (prints skills list with installed annotations)
- `scripts/list-skills.py --format json`
- Example (experimental list): `scripts/list-skills.py --path skills/.experimental`
- `scripts/install-skill-from-github.py --repo <owner>/<repo> --path <path/to/skill> [<path/to/skill> ...]`
- `scripts/install-skill-from-github.py --url https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/tree/<ref>/<path>`
- Example (experimental skill): `scripts/install-skill-from-github.py --repo openai/skills --path skills/.experimental/<skill-name>`

## Behavior and Options

- Defaults to direct download for public GitHub repos.
- If download fails with auth/permission errors, falls back to git sparse checkout.
- Aborts if the destination skill directory already exists.
- Installs into `$CODEX_HOME/skills/<skill-name>` (defaults to `~/.codex/skills`).
- Multiple `--path` values install multiple skills in one run, each named from the path basename unless `--name` is supplied.
- Options: `--ref <ref>` (default `main`), `--dest <path>`, `--method auto|download|git`.

## Notes

- Curated listing is fetched from `https://github.com/openai/skills/tree/main/skills/.curated` via the GitHub API. If it is unavailable, explain the error and exit.
- Private GitHub repos can be accessed via existing git credentials or optional `GITHUB_TOKEN`/`GH_TOKEN` for download.
- Git fallback tries HTTPS first, then SSH.
- The skills at https://github.com/openai/skills/tree/main/skills/.system are preinstalled, so no need to help users install those. If they ask, just explain this. If they insist, you can download and overwrite.
- Installed annotations come from `$CODEX_HOME/skills`.

name: dispatching-parallel-agents
description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
---

# Dispatching Parallel Agents

## Overview

You delegate tasks to specialized agents with isolated context. By precisely crafting their instructions and context, you ensure they stay focused and succeed at their task. They should never inherit your session's context or history — you construct exactly what they need. This also preserves your own context for coordination work.

When you have multiple unrelated failures (different test files, different subsystems, different bugs), investigating them sequentially wastes time. Each investigation is independent and can happen in parallel.

**Core principle:** Dispatch one agent per independent problem domain. Let them work concurrently.

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Multiple failures?" [shape=diamond];
    "Are they independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Single agent investigates all" [shape=box];
    "One agent per problem domain" [shape=box];
    "Can they work in parallel?" [shape=diamond];
    "Sequential agents" [shape=box];
    "Parallel dispatch" [shape=box];

    "Multiple failures?" -> "Are they independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Are they independent?" -> "Single agent investigates all" [label="no - related"];
    "Are they independent?" -> "Can they work in parallel?" [label="yes"];
    "Can they work in parallel?" -> "Parallel dispatch" [label="yes"];
    "Can they work in parallel?" -> "Sequential agents" [label="no - shared state"];
}
```

**Use when:**
- 3+ test files failing with different root causes
- Multiple subsystems broken independently
- Each problem can be understood without context from others
- No shared state between investigations

**Don't use when:**
- Failures are related (fix one might fix others)
- Need to understand full system state
- Agents would interfere with each other

## The Pattern

### 1. Identify Independent Domains

Group failures by what's broken:
- File A tests: Tool approval flow
- File B tests: Batch completion behavior
- File C tests: Abort functionality

Each domain is independent - fixing tool approval doesn't affect abort tests.

### 2. Create Focused Agent Tasks

Each agent gets:
- **Specific scope:** One test file or subsystem
- **Clear goal:** Make these tests pass
- **Constraints:** Don't change other code
- **Expected output:** Summary of what you found and fixed

### 3. Dispatch in Parallel

```typescript
// In Claude Code / AI environment
Task("Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts failures")
// All three run concurrently
```

### 4. Review and Integrate

When agents return:
- Read each summary
- Verify fixes don't conflict
- Run full test suite
- Integrate all changes

## Agent Prompt Structure

Good agent prompts are:
1. **Focused** - One clear problem domain
2. **Self-contained** - All context needed to understand the problem
3. **Specific about output** - What should the agent return?

```markdown
Fix the 3 failing tests in src/agents/agent-tool-abort.test.ts:

1. "should abort tool with partial output capture" - expects 'interrupted at' in message
2. "should handle mixed completed and aborted tools" - fast tool aborted instead of completed
3. "should properly track pendingToolCount" - expects 3 results but gets 0

These are timing/race condition issues. Your task:

1. Read the test file and understand what each test verifies
2. Identify root cause - timing issues or actual bugs?
3. Fix by:
   - Replacing arbitrary timeouts with event-based waiting
   - Fixing bugs in abort implementation if found
   - Adjusting test expectations if testing changed behavior

Do NOT just increase timeouts - find the real issue.

Return: Summary of what you found and what you fixed.
```

## Common Mistakes

**❌ Too broad:** "Fix all the tests" - agent gets lost
**✅ Specific:** "Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts" - focused scope

**❌ No context:** "Fix the race condition" - agent doesn't know where
**✅ Context:** Paste the error messages and test names

**❌ No constraints:** Agent might refactor everything
**✅ Constraints:** "Do NOT change production code" or "Fix tests only"

**❌ Vague output:** "Fix it" - you don't know what changed
**✅ Specific:** "Return summary of root cause and changes"

## When NOT to Use

**Related failures:** Fixing one might fix others - investigate together first
**Need full context:** Understanding requires seeing entire system
**Exploratory debugging:** You don't know what's broken yet
**Shared state:** Agents would interfere (editing same files, using same resources)

## Real Example from Session

**Scenario:** 6 test failures across 3 files after major refactoring

**Failures:**
- agent-tool-abort.test.ts: 3 failures (timing issues)
- batch-completion-behavior.test.ts: 2 failures (tools not executing)
- tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts: 1 failure (execution count = 0)

**Decision:** Independent domains - abort logic separate from batch completion separate from race conditions

**Dispatch:**
```
Agent 1 → Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts
Agent 2 → Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts
Agent 3 → Fix tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts
```

**Results:**
- Agent 1: Replaced timeouts with event-based waiting
- Agent 2: Fixed event structure bug (threadId in wrong place)
- Agent 3: Added wait for async tool execution to complete

**Integration:** All fixes independent, no conflicts, full suite green

**Time saved:** 3 problems solved in parallel vs sequentially

## Key Benefits

1. **Parallelization** - Multiple investigations happen simultaneously
2. **Focus** - Each agent has narrow scope, less context to track
3. **Independence** - Agents don't interfere with each other
4. **Speed** - 3 problems solved in time of 1

## Verification

After agents return:
1. **Review each summary** - Understand what changed
2. **Check for conflicts** - Did agents edit same code?
3. **Run full suite** - Verify all fixes work together
4. **Spot check** - Agents can make systematic errors

## Real-World Impact

From debugging session (2025-10-03):
- 6 failures across 3 files
- 3 agents dispatched in parallel
- All investigations completed concurrently
- All fixes integrated successfully
- Zero conflicts between agent changes

# Skill Installer

Helps install skills. By default these are from https://github.com/openai/skills/tree/main/skills/.curated, but users can also provide other locations. Experimental skills live in https://github.com/openai/skills/tree/main/skills/.experimental and can be installed the same way.

- List skills when the user asks what is available, or if the user uses this skill without specifying what to do. Default listing is `.curated`, but you can pass `--path skills/.experimental` when they ask about experimental skills.
- Install from the curated list when the user provides a skill name.
- Install from another repo when the user provides a GitHub repo/path (including private repos).

status.completed:
奉命清点。以下是我作为赫尔墨斯给奥林匹斯神系做的 Codex skill 候选映射：每位神保留一个“主 skill”，若干“辅 skill”，以及适合被召唤的触发条件。

| 神祇 | 主 skill | 辅 skill | 触发条件 |
|---|---|---|---|
| 宙斯 Zeus | `architecture-decision` | `architecture-review`, `prd`, `project-stage-detect` | 需要拍板重大技术方向、统一系统原则、决定架构取舍、给项目定调时 |
| 赫拉 Hera | `release-checklist` | `launch-checklist`, `milestone-review`, `sprint-status` | 需要治理秩序、发布前总检、跨团队状态对齐、确保流程完整时 |
| 波塞冬 Poseidon | `systematic-debugging` | `perf-profile`, `smoke-check`, `regression-suite` | 系统不稳定、故障像海啸一样扩散、性能波动、回归风险高时 |
| 得墨忒耳 Demeter | `content-audit` | `localize`, `changelog`, `patch-notes` | 内容资产、文案、版本材料、翻译与产出物需要盘点和培育时 |
| 雅典娜 Athena | `writing-plans` | `test-driven-development`, `architecture-review`, `code-review` | 需要理性拆解复杂任务、先谋后动、制定实施路径或审视方案质量时 |
| 阿波罗 Apollo | `web-design-guidelines` | `ui-design`, `ui-ux-pro-max`, `ckm:design` | 需要审美、清晰度、前端体验、可访问性、视觉秩序与界面判断时 |
| 阿尔忒弥斯 Artemis | `security-audit` | `regression-suite`, `bug-triage`, `smoke-check` | 需要猎杀隐患、发现漏洞、保护边界、识别潜在风险时 |
| 阿瑞斯 Ares | `bug-report` | `bug-triage`, `systematic-debugging`, `smoke-check` | 线上战况紧急、缺陷爆发、需要快速定位战场并组织修复时 |
| 阿佛洛狄忒 Aphrodite | `ckm:brand` | `ckm:ui-styling`, `ui-ux-pro-max`, `ckm:design` | 需要品牌表达、视觉魅力、界面气质、文案与美感统一时 |
| 赫菲斯托斯 Hephaestus | `test-driven-development` | `perf-profile`, `tech-debt`, `reverse-document` | 需要扎实实现、补测试、修底层结构、清理技术债、从代码反推文档时 |
| 赫尔墨斯 Hermes | `dispatching-parallel-agents` | `subagent-driven-development`, `executing-plans`, `openai-docs` | 需要分派任务、并行推进、跨工具协调、查官方资料、传递上下文时 |
| 狄俄尼索斯 Dionysus | `brainstorming` | `prd`, `ui-design`, `ckm:slides` | 需要创意发散、产品概念、原型灵感、故事化表达或演示叙事时 |
| 赫斯提亚 Hestia | `verification-before-completion` | `smoke-check`, `regression-suite`, `finishing-a-development-branch` | 交付前守炉火：确认工作真的完成、测试通过、分支可收束时 |
| 哈迪斯 Hades | `tech-debt` | `reverse-document`, `content-audit`, `security-audit` | 需要深入遗留系统、盘点阴影资产、处理旧债、挖出被埋的问题时 |
| 墨提斯 Metis | `prd` | `writing-plans`, `architecture-decision`, `self-improving-agent` | 需要智慧型产品判断、需求成形、策略拆解、让系统从经验中学习时 |
| 司衡 Themis / Dike | `code-review` | `requesting-code-review`, `receiving-code-review`, `qa-plan` | 需要裁断质量、公平审查、处理评审意见、建立验收标准时 |

补充几个“神职归类”：

| 职能 | 推荐 skill 组合 |
|---|---|
| 开始复杂任务 | `brainstorming` → `writing-plans` → `test-driven-development` |
| 实施已有计划 | `executing-plans` → `subagent-driven-development` → `verification-before-completion` |
| 修 bug | `bug-report` → `systematic-debugging` → `regression-suite` |

status.completed:
宙斯，我建议把规则定成“职能优先、最小挂载、任务触发、事后校验”，避免奥林匹斯变成一座到处贴符咒的行政神庙。

**一、基本原则**

1. **神职稳定，skill 流动**
   神的核心身份不由 skill 定义。skill 只是处理特定事务时的工具，不应反过来改写神职。

2. **常驻 skill 只给高频、核心、低风险误用的职责**
   如果某个能力几乎每次履职都需要，且不会诱导过度流程化，才允许常驻。

3. **任务 skill 按需启用，用完卸下**
   复杂、低频、强流程、强审查类 skill 不常驻，只在对应任务出现时启用。

4. **不得为了显得郑重而启用 skill**
   仪式感不是治理质量。凡是普通判断能解决的事，不得召唤三层流程、五份模板、七位见证神。

---

**二、必须常驻的 skill**

这些是“神职本体工具”，应默认挂载。

| 神祇 | 必挂 skill | 理由 |
|---|---|---|
| 宙斯 | `governance`, `delegation`, `conflict-resolution` | 最高裁断、授权、处置神间争端 |
| 墨提斯 / 司衡 | `policy-review`, `risk-assessment`, `consistency-check` | 宙斯之下最高审定者，负责规则稳健性 |
| 雅典娜 | `strategy`, `architecture-review`, `decision-record` | 战略、制度、结构性判断 |
| 赫拉 | `protocol`, `legitimacy-review`, `stakeholder-management` | 秩序、名分、婚姻与联盟合法性 |
| 阿波罗 | `truth-verification`, `prophecy-interpretation`, `arts-review` | 真理、预言、医艺与秩序之光 |
| 阿尔忒弥斯 | `boundary-protection`, `wilderness-operations`, `youth-safeguarding` | 边界、野地、少女与幼者保护 |
| 阿瑞斯 | `combat-readiness`, `threat-response` | 战争执行与冲突爆发处置 |
| 赫菲斯托斯 | `engineering`, `artifact-audit`, `craft-quality` | 锻造、神器、系统可靠性 |
| 赫尔墨斯 | `messaging`, `routing`, `negotiation` | 传令、贸易、跨域沟通 |
| 德墨忒尔 | `resource-stewardship`, `seasonal-planning` | 农业、粮食、周期治理 |
| 波塞冬 | `domain-control`, `disaster-response`, `navigation-risk` | 海洋、地震、航行风险 |
| 哈迪斯 | `custody`, `records-integrity`, `boundary-enforcement` | 冥界秩序、亡者归属、不可逆边界 |
| 赫斯提亚 | `continuity`, `domestic-stability`, `ritual-minimalism` | 炉火、家宅、共同体稳定 |

---

**三、只在特定任务启用的 skill**

这些 skill 容易制造流程膨胀，不应常驻。

| skill 类型 | 启用条件 |
|---|---|
| `crisis-management` | 神域失控、凡间大灾、众神冲突升级 |
| `legal-adjudication` | 涉及誓言、契约、惩罚、继承、神权边界 |
| `security-audit` | 神器、圣所、神谕、冥界通道、奥林匹斯权限异常 |
| `war-room` | 多神参与的战争、远征、围城、巨人或提丰级威胁 |
| `diplomacy` | 神族、凡人城邦、异域神系之间的正式谈判 |
| `artifact-design` | 新造神器、改造神器、封印物、自动装置 |
| `prophecy-review` | 预言影响重大决策，或存在歧义、诱导、悖论 |
| `succession-review` | 权柄转移、神职继承、半神升格 |
| `retrospective` | 重大事件之后复盘，不用于日常小事 |
| `documentation` | 只有当决定会被后续引用、执行或争议时才记录 |

---

**四、避免过度安装**

采用“三问门槛”：

1. **没有这个 skill，会不会明显降低判断质量？**
2. **这个 skill 是否对应当前神的长期神职，而不是临时兴趣？**
3. **常驻它会不会诱导该神把简单事务复杂化？**

三问中有两问是否定，就不常驻。

再加一条硬规则：

**每位神常驻 skill 不超过 3 个。**  
除宙斯、墨提斯、雅典娜外，不得突破。突破必须由司衡批准，并注明期限。

---

**五、避免过度仪式化**

设三档事务等级：

1. **轻事务**
   单一神职范围内、影响小、可逆。  
   直接处理，不启用额外 skill，不写长文档。

2. **中事务**
   跨两个神域、影响一城一族、部分不可逆。  
   启用 1 个任务 skill，必要时请司衡快速审看。

3. **重事务**
   跨三位以上主神、影响神权结构、战争、死亡、誓言、灾变。  
   启用任务 skill 组合，必须有宙斯或司衡裁定。

禁止事项：

- 不为每个决定写 ADR。
- 不为每次争执开审判庭。
- 不为日常神职启用危机流程。
- 不允许“因为有 skill 所以寻找任务”。

---

**六、没有对应 skill 的神职如何处理**

采用“神职先行，skill 后置”。

1. **先描述神职**
   例如酒神、梦神、复仇女神、命运女神，不急着套现成 skill。

2. **判断任务是否已有邻近 skill**
   梦境可临时用 `interpretation`；复仇可用 `justice-review`；酒神可用 `ecstasy-risk` 或 `social-dynamics`。

3. **如果没有邻近 skill，就不硬装**
   神可以凭神职处理事务。skill 不是合法性的来源。

4. **只有重复出现三次以上，才创建新 skill**
   新 skill 必须写明：
   - 适用神职
   - 启用条件
   - 禁用场景
   - 可能造成的滥用
   - 由谁审定

---

**七、推荐治理流程**

1. **宙斯定方向**
   规定哪些事务属于全奥林匹斯级别。

2. **墨提斯 / 司衡管边界**
   审核 skill 是否过度、是否侵入他神神职、是否制造流程负担。

3. **各神保留自治**
   本神域内的小事，不需向奥林匹斯申请 skill。

4. **季度清理**
   每隔一段时间检查：
   - 哪些 skill 从未使用
   - 哪些 skill 被滥用
   - 哪些神职没有工具但运转良好
   - 哪些任务反复出现，值得沉淀新 skill

---

**八、最终规则一句话**

**主神常驻少量本职 skill，复杂事务按需临时启用，跨域重大事项交由宙斯裁断、司衡审定；没有对应 skill 的神职保持神职自治，不为工具而重塑神明。**

019e1c7d-6ae7-76f2-ab9f-d4e1c0e3697e.completed:
宙斯，我建议把规则定成“职能优先、最小挂载、任务触发、事后校验”，避免奥林匹斯变成一座到处贴符咒的行政神庙。

**一、基本原则**

1. **神职稳定，skill 流动**
   神的核心身份不由 skill 定义。skill 只是处理特定事务时的工具，不应反过来改写神职。

2. **常驻 skill 只给高频、核心、低风险误用的职责**
   如果某个能力几乎每次履职都需要，且不会诱导过度流程化，才允许常驻。

3. **任务 skill 按需启用，用完卸下**
   复杂、低频、强流程、强审查类 skill 不常驻，只在对应任务出现时启用。

4. **不得为了显得郑重而启用 skill**
   仪式感不是治理质量。凡是普通判断能解决的事，不得召唤三层流程、五份模板、七位见证神。

---

**二、必须常驻的 skill**

这些是“神职本体工具”，应默认挂载。

| 神祇 | 必挂 skill | 理由 |
|---|---|---|
| 宙斯 | `governance`, `delegation`, `conflict-resolution` | 最高裁断、授权、处置神间争端 |
| 墨提斯 / 司衡 | `policy-review`, `risk-assessment`, `consistency-check` | 宙斯之下最高审定者，负责规则稳健性 |
| 雅典娜 | `strategy`, `architecture-review`, `decision-record` | 战略、制度、结构性判断 |
| 赫拉 | `protocol`, `legitimacy-review`, `stakeholder-management` | 秩序、名分、婚姻与联盟合法性 |
| 阿波罗 | `truth-verification`, `prophecy-interpretation`, `arts-review` | 真理、预言、医艺与秩序之光 |
| 阿尔忒弥斯 | `boundary-protection`, `wilderness-operations`, `youth-safeguarding` | 边界、野地、少女与幼者保护 |
| 阿瑞斯 | `combat-readiness`, `threat-response` | 战争执行与冲突爆发处置 |
| 赫菲斯托斯 | `engineering`, `artifact-audit`, `craft-quality` | 锻造、神器、系统可靠性 |
| 赫尔墨斯 | `messaging`, `routing`, `negotiation` | 传令、贸易、跨域沟通 |
| 德墨忒尔 | `resource-stewardship`, `seasonal-planning` | 农业、粮食、周期治理 |
| 波塞冬 | `domain-control`, `disaster-response`, `navigation-risk` | 海洋、地震、航行风险 |
| 哈迪斯 | `custody`, `records-integrity`, `boundary-enforcement` | 冥界秩序、亡者归属、不可逆边界 |
| 赫斯提亚 | `continuity`, `domestic-stability`, `ritual-minimalism` | 炉火、家宅、共同体稳定 |

---

**三、只在特定任务启用的 skill**

这些 skill 容易制造流程膨胀，不应常驻。

| skill 类型 | 启用条件 |
|---|---|
| `crisis-management` | 神域失控、凡间大灾、众神冲突升级 |
| `legal-adjudication` | 涉及誓言、契约、惩罚、继承、神权边界 |
| `security-audit` | 神器、圣所、神谕、冥界通道、奥林匹斯权限异常 |
| `war-room` | 多神参与的战争、远征、围城、巨人或提丰级威胁 |
| `diplomacy` | 神族、凡人城邦、异域神系之间的正式谈判 |
| `artifact-design` | 新造神器、改造神器、封印物、自动装置 |
| `prophecy-review` | 预言影响重大决策，或存在歧义、诱导、悖论 |
| `succession-review` | 权柄转移、神职继承、半神升格 |
| `retrospective` | 重大事件之后复盘，不用于日常小事 |
| `documentation` | 只有当决定会被后续引用、执行或争议时才记录 |

---

**四、避免过度安装**

采用“三问门槛”：

1. **没有这个 skill，会不会明显降低判断质量？**
2. **这个 skill 是否对应当前神的长期神职，而不是临时兴趣？**
3. **常驻它会不会诱导该神把简单事务复杂化？**

三问中有两问是否定，就不常驻。

再加一条硬规则：

**每位神常驻 skill 不超过 3 个。**  
除宙斯、墨提斯、雅典娜外，不得突破。突破必须由司衡批准，并注明期限。

---

**五、避免过度仪式化**

设三档事务等级：

1. **轻事务**
   单一神职范围内、影响小、可逆。  
   直接处理，不启用额外 skill，不写长文档。

2. **中事务**
   跨两个神域、影响一城一族、部分不可逆。  
   启用 1 个任务 skill，必要时请司衡快速审看。

3. **重事务**
   跨三位以上主神、影响神权结构、战争、死亡、誓言、灾变。  
   启用任务 skill 组合，必须有宙斯或司衡裁定。

禁止事项：

- 不为每个决定写 ADR。
- 不为每次争执开审判庭。
- 不为日常神职启用危机流程。
- 不允许“因为有 skill 所以寻找任务”。

---

采用“神职先行，skill 后置”。

   例如酒神、梦神、复仇女神、命运女神，不急着套现成 skill。

2. **判断任务是否已有邻近 skill**
   梦境可临时用 `interpretation`；复仇可用 `justice-review`；酒神可用 `ecstasy-risk` 或 `social-dynamics`。

3. **如果没有邻近 skill，就不硬装**
   神可以凭神职处理事务。skill 不是合法性的来源。

4. **只有重复出现三次以上，才创建新 skill**
   新 skill 必须写明：

   - 启用条件
   - 禁用场景
   - 可能造成的滥用
   - 由谁审定

   规定哪些事务属于全奥林匹斯级别。

3. **各神保留自治**
   本神域内的小事，不需向奥林匹斯申请 skill。

4. **季度清理**
   每隔一段时间检查：

   - 哪些 skill 被滥用

   - 哪些任务反复出现，值得沉淀新 skill

| `artifact-design` | 新造神器、改造神器、封印物、自动装置 |
| `prophecy-review` | 预言影响重大决策，或存在歧义、诱导、悖论 |
| `succession-review` | 权柄转移、神职继承、半神升格 |
| `retrospective` | 重大事件之后复盘，不用于日常小事 |
| `documentation` | 只有当决定会被后续引用、执行或争议时才记录 |

---

**四、避免过度安装**

采用“三问门槛”：

1. **没有这个 skill，会不会明显降低判断质量？**
2. **这个 skill 是否对应当前神的长期神职，而不是临时兴趣？**
3. **常驻它会不会诱导该神把简单事务复杂化？**

三问中有两问是否定，就不常驻。

再加一条硬规则：

**每位神常驻 skill 不超过 3 个。**  
除宙斯、墨提斯、雅典娜外，不得突破。突破必须由司衡批准，并注明期限。

---

**五、避免过度仪式化**

设三档事务等级：

1. **轻事务**
   单一神职范围内、影响小、可逆。  
   直接处理，不启用额外 skill，不写长文档。

2. **中事务**
   跨两个神域、影响一城一族、部分不可逆。  
   启用 1 个任务 skill，必要时请司衡快速审看。

3. **重事务**
   跨三位以上主神、影响神权结构、战争、死亡、誓言、灾变。  
   启用任务 skill 组合，必须有宙斯或司衡裁定。

禁止事项：

- 不为每个决定写 ADR。
- 不为每次争执开审判庭。
- 不为日常神职启用危机流程。
- 不允许“因为有 skill 所以寻找任务”。

---

**六、没有对应 skill 的神职如何处理**

采用“神职先行，skill 后置”。

1. **先描述神职**
   例如酒神、梦神、复仇女神、命运女神，不急着套现成 skill。

2. **判断任务是否已有邻近 skill**
   梦境可临时用 `interpretation`；复仇可用 `justice-review`；酒神可用 `ecstasy-risk` 或 `social-dynamics`。

3. **如果没有邻近 skill，就不硬装**
   神可以凭神职处理事务。skill 不是合法性的来源。

4. **只有重复出现三次以上，才创建新 skill**
   新 skill 必须写明：
   - 适用神职
   - 启用条件
   - 禁用场景
   - 可能造成的滥用
   - 由谁审定

---

**七、推荐治理流程**

1. **宙斯定方向**
   规定哪些事务属于全奥林匹斯级别。

2. **墨提斯 / 司衡管边界**
   审核 skill 是否过度、是否侵入他神神职、是否制造流程负担。

3. **各神保留自治**
   本神域内的小事，不需向奥林匹斯申请 skill。

4. **季度清理**
   每隔一段时间检查：
   - 哪些 skill 从未使用
   - 哪些 skill 被滥用
   - 哪些神职没有工具但运转良好
   - 哪些任务反复出现，值得沉淀新 skill

---

**八、最终规则一句话**

**主神常驻少量本职 skill，复杂事务按需临时启用，跨域重大事项交由宙斯裁断、司衡审定；没有对应 skill 的神职保持神职自治，不为工具而重塑神明。**"}}
</subagent_notification>

# 奥林匹斯 Skill 装配表

日期：2026-05-12

## 总则

宙斯定意志，司衡 / 墨提斯掌裁决，诸神司其职。Skill 是神职的工具，不是神职本身。

装配原则：

- 只装配当前 Codex 真实可用的 skill。
- 每位神保留 1 个主 skill，辅 skill 按任务触发。
- 复杂、高风险、跨域任务必须召神；低风险、路径清楚的小事由司衡直接处理。
- 不为了显得郑重而启用 skill。
- 没有合适 skill 时，按神职处理，不硬造工具名。

## 最高神格

| 神格 | 主责 | 主 skill | 辅 skill |
|---|---|---|---|
| 宙斯 | 最终裁决、授权、方向拍板 | 无需装配 | `architecture-decision`, `prd`, `project-stage-detect` 仅在需要形成正式决策材料时由司衡代用 |
| 司衡 / 墨提斯 | 最高调度、判断、封驳、验收、整合 | `code-review` | `dispatching-parallel-agents`, `subagent-driven-development`, `writing-plans`, `verification-before-completion`, `self-improving-agent` |

## 十二主神

| 神祇 | 主责 | 主 skill | 辅 skill |
|---|---|---|---|
| 雅典娜 | 架构、策略、复杂拆解、技术取舍 | `writing-plans` | `architecture-review`, `architecture-decision`, `brainstorming`, `code-review` |
| 赫菲斯托斯 | 代码实现、重构、工具链、构建 | `test-driven-development` | `systematic-debugging`, `perf-profile`, `tech-debt`, `reverse-document` |
| 阿波罗 | UI/UX、前端体验、视觉秩序、可用性 | `web-design-guidelines` | `ui-design`, `ui-ux-pro-max`, `ckm:ui-styling`, `ckm:design` |
| 阿佛洛狄忒 | 品牌、美感、视觉气质、情绪吸引力 | `ckm:brand` | `ckm:design`, `ckm:ui-styling`, `ui-ux-pro-max` |
| 阿尔忒弥斯 | 测试验证、回归、质量猎检、边界条件 | `regression-suite` | `smoke-check`, `qa-plan`, `test-evidence-review`, `bug-triage` |
| 阿瑞斯 | 紧急故障、安全攻防、高压缺陷 | `systematic-debugging` | `security-audit`, `bug-report`, `bug-triage`, `smoke-check` |
| 赫拉 | 产品秩序、范围控制、优先级、发布边界 | `prd` | `scope-check`, `milestone-review`, `sprint-plan`, `sprint-status`, `release-checklist` |
| 赫尔墨斯 | 调研、文档、接口、传信、并行协调 | `dispatching-parallel-agents` | `openai-docs`, `documents:documents`, `executing-plans`, `subagent-driven-development` |
| 得墨忒耳 | 数据、内容、知识资产、迁移、长期生长 | `content-audit` | `localize`, `spreadsheets:Spreadsheets`, `changelog`, `patch-notes` |
| 波塞冬 | 部署、基础设施、运行环境、性能容量 | `smoke-check` | `perf-profile`, `release-checklist`, `launch-checklist`, `verification-before-completion` |
| 赫斯提亚 | 日常维护、工程内务、收尾、稳定性 | `verification-before-completion` | `finishing-a-development-branch`, `tech-debt`, `regression-suite`, `smoke-check` |
| 狄俄尼索斯 | 创意、命名、文案、叙事、表达感染力 | `brainstorming` | `prd`, `ckm:slides`, `presentations:Presentations`, `ckm:brand` |
| 哈迪斯 | 遗留系统、隐藏依赖、归档、深层债务 | `tech-debt` | `reverse-document`, `security-audit`, `content-audit`, `architecture-review` |

## 召神规则

必须召神：

- 架构、长期路线、核心抽象：雅典娜。
- UI/UX、前端体验、视觉秩序：阿波罗；品牌气质另召阿佛洛狄忒。
- 代码实现、重构、复杂 bug：赫菲斯托斯。
- 测试、验收、回归风险：阿尔忒弥斯。
- 安全、权限、隐私、紧急故障：阿瑞斯。
- 产品范围、优先级、版本裁剪：赫拉。
- 调研、官方文档、接口资料：赫尔墨斯。
- 数据、内容、知识库、迁移：得墨忒耳。
- 部署、环境、性能、发布运行：波塞冬。
- 日常收尾、分支整理、完成前验证：赫斯提亚。
- 命名、文案、创意表达：狄俄尼索斯。
- 遗留系统、深层债务、隐藏依赖：哈迪斯。

司衡可直接处理：

- 简单问答、解释、总结。
- 单文件、低风险、路径明确的小改动。
- 已有方案下的机械执行。
- 小范围文案润色。
- 不涉及安全、部署、数据迁移、复杂 UI、长期架构的任务。

## 最小任务卡

```text
任务名：
宙斯意图：
任务类型：
主责神：
协作神：
挂载 skill：
范围边界：
完成标准：
风险点：
验证方式：
交付物：
司衡裁定：
```

## 过度仪式防线

- 一次默认只召一位主责神。
- 三位以上同时参与，只用于重大设计、事故、发布、数据迁移、安全风险。
- 每次召神前，司衡必须能回答：这位神会明显提高结果质量吗？
- 子 agent 的系统昵称不是神名；汇报时使用神名，必要时括号标注系统昵称。
